claim_levels.claim: Managed paths remain inside the workspace
claim_levels.limitation: new filesystem features must reuse these primitives

claim_levels.claim: Persistent creation is no-clobber
claim_levels.level: regression-proven for current writers and same-host commit races
claim_levels.limitation: ancestor substitution and power-loss durability remain unproven

claim_levels.claim: Package tools clean only owned output
claim_levels.limitation: other future destructive tools need their own ownership kind

claim_levels.claim: Instance layout is structurally isolated
claim_levels.limitation: real Factorio write semantics unproven

claim_levels.claim: Process boundary preserves intended isolation
claim_levels.limitation: proves FacMan and probe behavior, not Factorio internals

claim_levels.claim: Per-instance run ownership is exclusive
claim_levels.limitation: shared/network and multi-host filesystems remain unproven

claim_levels.claim: Reviewed local diagnostic export is bounded
claim_levels.limitation: no arbitrary formats, GUI UX, shared filesystems, or authenticity

claim_levels.claim: JSON contracts describe runtime output
claim_levels.limitation: remaining legacy commands and goldens are not implicitly promoted

claim_levels.claim: Command registry introspection matches dispatch
claim_levels.limitation: new descriptor versions and handlers require the same parity proof

claim_levels.claim: Run preview uses the authoritative route
claim_levels.limitation: no process starts; real Factorio remains unproven

claim_levels.claim: Windows install discovery is read-only
claim_levels.limitation: registry proof is host-dependent; macOS and Linux provider databases remain future work

claim_levels.claim: Windows x64 static CLI package runs after relocation
claim_levels.limitation: local Windows x64 only; unsigned and unpublished

claim_levels.claim: Linux x64 CLI tarball runs after relocation
claim_levels.limitation: Ubuntu 24.04 x64/glibc 2.39 only; rerun per revision; unsigned and unpublished

claim_levels.claim: macOS x64 CLI tarball runs after relocation
claim_levels.limitation: Intel runner and target 13.0 only; no Apple Silicon, AppKit runtime, signing, or publication claim

claim_levels.claim: Package verification is setup-authoritative
claim_levels.limitation: authenticity and setup mutation remain unproven

claim_levels.claim: Package hashes authenticate publisher
claim_levels.limitation: signatures or trusted external metadata required

claim_levels.claim: Package inputs and provenance are recorded
claim_levels.limitation: provider licenses are `NOASSERTION`; unsigned evidence is not publisher authentication

claim_levels.claim: Experimental public C ABI has a correctness floor
claim_levels.limitation: stable third-party compatibility and independent consumers remain unproven

claim_levels.claim: CLI uses only the client boundary
claim_levels.limitation: process and daemon transports remain unavailable

claim_levels.claim: Install-tree archives reproduce
claim_levels.limitation: other targets need per-revision CI; output is unsigned

claim_levels.claim: Normal AIDE context excludes history bulk
claim_levels.limitation: development governance only; no product or human proof

known_blockers: Real Factorio isolation remains operator-only and has no human verdict.
Linux sanitizer, libFuzzer, clang-tidy, coverage, and target package results are CI-owned per revision.
macOS target package proof is CI-owned per revision; AppKit remains compile-only.
Artifacts are unsigned and unpublished; integrity and provenance do not authenticate a publisher.
quarantined_capabilities: run.execute pending operator-supplied real Factorio verdict
release signing, notarization, publication, and publisher authenticity
truth_boundaries: AIDE is development governance only and never a product dependency.
Focused affected tests do not replace the full promotion matrix.
Human acceptance is never inferred from automated checks.
